hypothesis: Introducing a bulky aromatic residue at position 5 and a negatively charged residue at position 6 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6 while disrupting off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) electrostatic complementarity, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective in achieving strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient in ensuring selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5. Targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are necessary to disrupt conserved off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) where SSTR3/SSTR5 conserved-pocket contacts are likely occurring.
Propose mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the core pharmacophore (FWKT) or disulfide bonds (Cys3/Cys14).
